summary: Descriptive overview of necessitydefense in substantive law: choice of evils, legislative preclusion.
key_points: choice of evils is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for necessitydefense are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine necessitydefense in litigation.
The doctrine of necessitydefense has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding necessitydefense requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
